topic: Appellation rules, origin and market value

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A geographical indication defines an origin and a set of permitted production rules.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Traceability and compliance protect the place name and can create a recognizable market promise; broader categories normally allow more production flexibility.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The designation can support typicity, consumer recognition and perceived value without guaranteeing the quality of every bottle.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: origin rules protect a name and shape permitted production, while less restrictive categories allow greater flexibility.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
